introduction: This presentation provides a thorough analysis of our cybersecurity measures and practices, focusing particularly on our vulnerability scans conducted between January and September 2025.

subsections.heading: Cybersecurity Landscape in Virtual Healthcare
subsections.content:
• Rapid digitalization has increased the potential attack surface for cyber threats in the healthcare sector
• In 2024, the healthcare sector was the most targeted industry for cyber threats
• The advent of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has introduced new vulnerabilities
• Regulatory compliance, such as HIPAA and PIPEDA, are becoming more stringent
• Cybersecurity insurance is becoming a necessity for virtual healthcare providers
• Traditional security measures such as firewalls and antivirus software are no longer sufficient
• Investment in cybersecurity is projected to increase by 15% in 2026
subsections.justification: The slide discusses trends and issues in the cybersecurity landscape, but does not provide information on the number of vulnerability scans MediConn conducted in 2025.

subsections.heading: Vulnerability Scans: Q1-Q3 2025 Overview
subsections.content:
• Vulnerability scans are an essential part of our cybersecurity strategy
• We use a combination of automated and manual scans to ensure comprehensive coverage
• Our scans are designed to identify weaknesses in our network, systems, and applications
• We prioritize remediation efforts based on the severity of the vulnerabilities identified
• Our vulnerability scanning processes are compliant with industry standards and regulations
• In addition to scheduled scans, we conduct ad-hoc scans in response to specific threats or incidents
• Our cybersecurity team regularly reviews and updates our scanning tools and methodologies
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the process and importance of vulnerability scans, but does not reveal how many scans were conducted during the specified period.

subsections.heading: Improvements & Recommendations for Cybersecurity
subsections.content:
• We recommend implementing a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system
• Regular security awareness training for all staff is crucial
• We should consider adopting a zero-trust security model
• An Incident Response Plan (IRP) needs to be developed and regularly updated
• Implementing multi-factor authentication can significantly reduce the risk of unauthorized access
• Regular audits of our cybersecurity practices and systems are necessary
• We should explore the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in threat detection and response
subsections.justification: Though this slide discusses improvements and recommendations for cybersecurity, it doesn't provide specific data on the number of vulnerability scans conducted in 2025.

conclusion: The data analyzed shows our robust commitment to cybersecurity. Moving forward, we recommend increased frequency of vulnerability scans and continued investment in high-quality cybersecurity measures.
